records.ety: [Gr. 'ana` on; in comp., on, up, upwards.]

defs: A prefix in words from the Greek, denoting up, upward, throughout, backward, back, again, anew.
Of each; an equal quantity; as, wine and honey, ana (or, contracted, aa) two ounces, ij., that is, of wine and honey, each, two ounces.
quotes: An apothecary with a . . . long bill of anas.

records.ety: [The neut. pl. ending of Latin adjectives in -anus.]

defs: A suffix to names of persons or places, used to denote a collection of notable sayings, literary gossip, anecdotes, etc. Thus, Scaligerana is a book containing the sayings of Scaliger, Johnsoniana of Johnson, etc.
quotes: It has been said that the table-talk of Selden is worth all the ana of the Continent.
notes: Used also as a substantive; as, the French anas
